title: AptarGroup Hires New CEO
author: Vandana Singh
description: AptarGroup appointed Gael Touya as new CEO, succeeding Stephan Tanda who will retire by year-end. Touya, with over 30 years at Aptar and former President of Aptar Pharma, is expected to drive growth in drug delivery and consumer product dispensing. William Blair analyst views the internal promotion positively as signaling strategic continuity. However, the stock shows mixed technical signals with neutral RSI but bearish MACD, and shares were down 3.99% at the time of publication.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: While the internal CEO promotion is viewed favorably by analysts as indicating strategic continuity and stability, the stock shows mixed technical indicators (neutral RSI but bearish MACD) and was trading down 3.99% at publication. The positive leadership narrative is offset by near-term weakness and declining 12-month performance (-11.48%).

title: Pharmaceutical Packaging Market to Exceed $219B by 2031 as Sustainability & Injectable Drug Packaging Reshape Industry Dynamics, Says Mordor Intelligence
author: Mordor Intelligence
description: The global pharmaceutical packaging market is projected to grow from USD 154.78 billion in 2025 to USD 219.08 billion by 2031, with a CAGR of 5.94%. Growth is driven by increasing demand for injectable therapies, sustainable packaging solutions, and stricter regulatory traceability requirements. Key trends include the shift toward recyclable materials, adoption of prefilled syringes, and advanced barrier systems.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Listed as a major company in the pharmaceutical packaging market, positioned to benefit from growing demand for sustainable and innovative packaging solutions across the expanding market.
Major competitor in glass and ready-to-use container formats, well-positioned to capitalize on the shift toward advanced polymer and glass solutions for injectable therapies.
Specializes in prefilled syringes and injectable packaging solutions, directly benefiting from the expanding biologics pipeline and growing demand for convenient self-administration formats.
Involved in advanced packaging technologies and closures, positioned to benefit from innovation in smart packaging and RFID-enabled verification systems.
Major player in prefilled syringes and injectable drug delivery systems, well-positioned to capture growth from the expanding biologics and injectable therapies pipeline.
Major player in secondary packaging and sustainable materials, well-positioned to capitalize on the industry shift toward recyclable and low-carbon packaging solutions.
Key player in pharmaceutical packaging with exposure to growing injectable drug packaging segment and advanced barrier systems innovation.

title: Pharmaceutical Glass Packaging Market Forecast: Growth from USD 5.32 Billion in 2025 to USD 12.39 Billion by 2035
author: Towards Packaging
description: The global pharmaceutical glass packaging market is projected to grow from USD 5.32 billion in 2025 to USD 12.39 billion by 2035, with a CAGR of 8.82%. Growth is driven by rising demand for injectables, biologics, and GLP-1 therapies, alongside stringent EU regulatory requirements for sustainability and anti-counterfeiting measures. Europe, particularly Germany, is expected to be a major growth contributor.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Specializes in high-end borosilicate glass vials and prefillable syringes for sensitive biologics, positioned to benefit from growing demand for premium injectable packaging and biologics market expansion.
Offers comprehensive Type I, II, and III glass containers and specialty ready-to-fill syringes, well-positioned to capture market growth across multiple pharmaceutical glass packaging segments.
Focuses on high-precision glass tubing and the widely adopted EZ-fill ready-to-use packaging platform, positioned to benefit from rising demand for advanced injectable solutions.
Supplies large-scale molded glass containers with focus on durability and recyclability, positioned to benefit from market growth and sustainability-driven regulatory requirements.
Focuses on drug delivery devices and specialized closures integrated with primary glass containers, positioned to benefit from rising demand for advanced injectable solutions.

title: Trends in Caps and Closures Market 2025-35
author: Towards Packaging
description: The global caps and closures market is projected to grow from USD 85.2 billion in 2025 to USD 138.18 billion by 2034, with a CAGR of 5.52%. Growth is driven by rising demand for packaged food, beverages, and pharmaceuticals. Asia Pacific dominates the market while North America shows the fastest growth. Key trends include adoption of sustainable materials, smart closures, and tamper-evident designs.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Recently completed merger with Berry Global in April 2025 to create the sector's largest supplier of rigid and flexible closure solutions, strengthening market position and capabilities.
Acquired dispensing business of Albéa in 2020, expanding global footprint in pumps, sprayers, and foam dispensing solutions for personal care markets.
Listed as a major player in the caps and closures market but no specific recent developments or strategic moves mentioned in the article.
Specializes in high-tech dispensing closures with recent expansion of pharmaceutical portfolio through acquisition of Sommaplast in December 2025, positioning for growth in fast-growing segments.

title: Bulky Cosmetic Packaging Market Size, Trends. Manufacturers and Suppliers Data
author: Towards Packaging
description: The global bulky cosmetic packaging market is projected to expand substantially from 2025 to 2034, driven by demand for premium designs, sustainability trends, and innovations in packaging technologies across personal care and beauty sectors.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Offers sustainable and innovative packaging solutions aligned with market trends
Specializes in high-performance dispensing systems, which are identified as a fastest-growing segment
